title: Cinnamon at the threshold
subtitle: A brief folk ritual for inviting prosperity into the home at the front door.
excerpt: A short folk ritual from general prosperity traditions. Three pinches of cinnamon blown across the front threshold on the first day of a new month, or any morning you want to set a deliberate intention about what enters your space.
sourceNotes: Drawn from general European and Latin American folk traditions in which cinnamon is used at the threshold for prosperity and protection. No specific tradition is claimed. Public domain.

practice.whenToUse: Use on the first day of a new month, or any morning when you want a simple physical ritual to mark an intention about prosperity.

content.text: A brief folk ritual from general prosperity traditions. Cinnamon at the threshold is one of the simplest practices in the money-magic canon: inexpensive, takes under two minutes, and works as a physical anchor for an intention rather than a passive wish.
Ground cinnamon (a small pinch fits easily in one palm)
On the first day of a new month (or any morning you choose), open your front door.
Stand on the outside of the threshold, facing in.
Hold a small pinch of ground cinnamon in your palm. Say once: "Prosperity enters with me."
Blow the cinnamon across the threshold and into the house. Do this three times in total: three pinches, three breaths, same phrase.
Leave the cinnamon where it falls for the day. Sweep it up the same evening.
The physical act of blowing something across the threshold is the practice. It gives the intention a body. Say the phrase plainly, not with theatrical gravity.

Drawn from general European and Latin American folk traditions in which cinnamon is used at the threshold for prosperity and protection. No specific tradition is claimed or represented. The practice is in the public domain.
